key_insight: A non-overlapping domain decomposition method turns the coupled Stokes-Biot fluid–poroelastic problem into a parallelizable interface solve with Lagrange multipliers and local subdomain solves.
engineering_relevance: The method enables scalable simulation of fluid–structure interaction in porous media, which is important for biomechanics, geomechanics, and other mixed-flow/elasticity applications.
method: Split the domain into Stokes and Biot subdomains with Stokes-Stokes, Biot-Biot, and Stokes-Biot interfaces.
Enforce interface physics weakly using Lagrange multipliers for displacement, pressure, and normal stress/velocity continuity.
Reduce the global system by Schur complement to an interface problem and solve it with GMRES plus parallel local subdomain solves.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable mixed finite element discretization, positive definiteness of the interface operator, fields-of-value convergence analysis, and numerical experiments demonstrating performance.
watch_item: Implementation is nontrivial because the method depends on carefully chosen stable mixed finite elements and multiple interface multipliers to preserve well-posedness and convergence.
paper_title: Domain decomposition methods for the Stokes-Biot model of fluid-poroelastic structure interaction
